title: DelegationToken
description: AITP v0.1 delegation token. Single-hop is the v0.1 default (no `chain` field). Multi-hop chains (RFC-AITP-0011) are carried in the optional `chain` and `chain_hash` fields.

DelegationStep.description: One link in a multi-hop delegation chain (RFC-AITP-0011 §1.1). Same shape as `grant_proof`. At hop 0, `source_tct_jti` references A's original peer-issued TCT to B; at hops i>0 it is a fresh UUIDv4 used by `chain_hash` and per-hop revocation.

subject.description: AID receiving capabilities at this hop.

capabilities.description: Capabilities granted at this hop. MUST be a subset of the previous hop's capabilities (transitive scope, RFC-AITP-0011 §3).

expires_at.description: MUST be <= the previous hop's `expires_at` (transitive expiry).

signature.description: At hop 0: re-uses the source TCT's signature verbatim. At hops i>0: issuer's signature over the canonical step body excluding `signature` (RFC-AITP-0011 §3 step 4).

delegator.description: AID of the original grant issuer (A).

delegatee.description: AID of the agent receiving delegation (C).

issued_by.description: AID of the agent issuing this delegation (B).

audience.description: MUST equal delegator's AID. Prevents use at other verifiers.

scope.description: Capabilities delegated to C. MUST be a subset of grant_proof.capabilities.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp of expiry. MUST be <= grant_proof.expires_at.

issuer.description: AID of the original grant issuer (A) for single-hop. For multi-hop (RFC-AITP-0011), the most-recent hop's issuer.

subject.description: AID of the original grantee (B). MUST equal delegation.issued_by.

issued_at.description: Unix timestamp; copied verbatim from the source TCT's issued_at so the source TCT signing input can be reconstructed byte-for-byte. See RFC-AITP-0006 §3.1.

source_tct_jti.description: JTI of A's original TCT to B. Used for revocation lookup.

signature.description: A's signature over the source TCT body (reused verbatim from the source TCT's signature).

chain.description: OPTIONAL multi-hop chain (RFC-AITP-0011). Absent or empty for single-hop. Ordered oldest hop first; the most-recent hop remains in `grant_proof`. v0.1 implementations MUST reject any token where this is non-empty with `DELEGATION_MULTIHOP_NOT_SUPPORTED`.

chain_hash.description: REQUIRED if `chain` is non-empty (RFC-AITP-0011 §5). `base64url(sha256(canonical_json([chain[0].source_tct_jti, ..., chain[n-2].source_tct_jti])))`. Bound into the outer signature so a hop cannot be removed without invalidating it.

signature.description: B's signature over canonical DelegationToken JSON (excluding signature). For multi-hop, covers `chain_hash`.

extensions.description: Optional extension namespace per RFC-AITP-0001 §7 and RFC-AITP-0012. Unknown keys inside `extensions` MUST be ignored; unknown fields outside it are rejected by this schema.
